page: Amazon Web Services m6a.8xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32 dedicated virtual CPUs, which correspond to 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core, and 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz. This configuration yields a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance and PassMark CPU encryption tests ranking in the top 25%. Memory subsystem performance is also strong, as evidenced by the PassMark memory score. However, multi-core scaling and overall multi-threaded execution, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core tests, fall into the average tier. Large-block memory read and write bandwidth benchmarks also align with average performance profiles.

From a resource density and cost-efficiency perspective, the m6a.8xlarge offers a balanced allocation of compute and memory resources without the added cost of bundled local NVMe storage or specialized GPU hardware. This makes it a balanced option for workloads that do not require high-speed local disk I/O. Appropriate workloads include general-purpose web serving, database operations, software compilation, and moderate-scale text or image processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m6a.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance and memory scores, alongside average multi-core scaling, average memory bandwidth, and average LLM inference speeds. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring stable compute and memory distribution without local storage overhead. It is suitable for general-purpose applications, database operations, software compilation, and web serving. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AES, and SHA_NI, which enhance cryptographic and integer calculation performance.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, and 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, delivering strong single-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and strong single-core performance on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and strong single-core execution for non-storage-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory provide a balanced 1:4 resource ratio.
Powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor.
Strong single-core CPU performance and memory subsystem benchmarks.
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached resources.
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for external data transfer.
Suited for general-purpose web serving, database operations, and software compilation.
